waveDependencies.note: Wave 4 needs security fixes (W0) and consolidated utilities (W2). Wave 5 additionally needs file splits (W3) since many correctness tasks touch split files.

verificationGate.policy: All four must pass after each wave before advancing.

testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Make importBackup safe — add caps, chunk inserts, validate payload
tasks.description: importBackup first calls deleteAllUserData (loops deleting 200 rows at a time), then iterates the uploaded payload inserting every row with no cap. The validator uses v.array(v.any()) with no length limit. A large or mid-failure import leaves partial data with the user's real data already deleted. Fix: (1) Add array length caps per table in the backup payload validator (max 10,000 log entries, proportional caps for other tables). (2) Split import into multiple mutations: delete phase first, then insert in chunks of ~200 rows per table via ctx.scheduler.runAfter so each chunk is its own transaction and partial failure is recoverable. (3) Add a client-side validation pass over data.logs checking each entry has a known type, numeric timestamp, and object data field. Reject the import if any log fails validation. After W3-01, this code lives in convex/backup.ts. Read convex/_generated/ai/guidelines.md first.
acceptanceCriteria: importBackup validator has per-table array length caps (max 10,000 for logs)
Import is chunked via ctx.scheduler.runAfter (each chunk its own transaction)
Partial failure does not lose all user data — delete + insert are separate phases
Client-side validateBackupPayload checks type, timestamp, and data fields per log
Import rejected if any log fails basic validation
Typecheck passes
All existing tests pass

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Cap exportBackup parallel table collects
tasks.description: exportBackup uses Promise.all to run 13 listRowsByUserId calls simultaneously, each calling .collect() with no row limit. Active users with years of data will hit Convex execution limits. Fix: (1) Add per-table row caps (e.g., 10,000 rows via .take(10000)). (2) For complete backup of large datasets, consider converting to a Convex action with higher execution limits. (3) Add a warning in the export result if any table was truncated. After W3-01, this code lives in convex/backup.ts.
acceptanceCriteria: exportBackup has per-table row caps (10,000)
Truncation is flagged in the export result or logged
Export does not crash for active users with large datasets
Typecheck passes

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Optimize buildFoodEvidenceResult — cap inputs and fix O(TxE) loop
tasks.description: buildFoodEvidenceResult in shared/foodEvidence.ts iterates args.logs four times and calls buildFoodTrials twice. findTriggerCorrelations has an O(T x E) inner loop. For a heavy user (1000 trials, 400 events) this runs 400,000 iterations inside a useQuery subscription. Fix: (1) Cap args.logs to the most recent 2,000 entries at the top of the function. (2) Change findTriggerCorrelations from O(T x E) to O(T + E) using a two-pointer approach — both arrays are sorted by timestamp, so scan through both in order.
acceptanceCriteria: buildFoodEvidenceResult caps input logs at 2,000 entries
findTriggerCorrelations uses two-pointer approach (O(T+E) not O(TxE))
Behavioral output is identical for datasets under 2,000 logs
Typecheck passes
All foodEvidence tests pass

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Add limit argument to conversations.listByDateRange
tasks.description: listByDateRange uses .collect() with no document limit. The client passes STABLE_END = 9_999_999_999_999 (year 2286). Over time this becomes unbounded. Fix: (1) Add a limit: v.optional(v.number()) arg with default 500. (2) Apply .take(limit) before .collect(). (3) Update the client-side call in ConversationPanel.tsx to pass a reasonable cap (200-500). Read convex/_generated/ai/guidelines.md first.
acceptanceCriteria: listByDateRange accepts a limit argument (default 500)
.take(limit) applied before .collect()
Client passes explicit limit (200-500)
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Skip FoodMatchingModal query on cold open
tasks.description: FoodMatchingModal.tsx (~L95-103) immediately fires a Convex useQuery requesting 160 results when the modal opens with no search query and no bucket. This is a subscription that re-fires on any food table mutation. Fix: Use 'skip' for the initial no-query, no-bucket state: pass 'skip' as the second argument to useQuery when both searchQuery is empty and activeBucket is null. Only fire the query once the user has typed at least 1 character or selected a bucket.
acceptanceCriteria: FoodMatchingModal uses 'skip' on cold open (no query, no bucket)
Query fires after 1+ character typed or bucket selected
Modal still shows results when user interacts
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Merge FoodFilterView double frequency scan into single memo
tasks.description: FoodFilterView.tsx has two useMemo blocks — frequentFoods (L95-115) and frequencyCountMap (L118-135) — that both scan the full logs array with identical dependency arrays. Fix: Combine into a single useMemo that returns { sortedFrequentFoods, countMap } from one pass over the logs array. The 14-day window can have thousands of entries; halving the scans helps.
acceptanceCriteria: Single useMemo computes both frequentFoods and countMap
Logs array is scanned exactly once (not twice)
Output values are identical to the two-memo version

tasks.title: Break mergeDuplicates into phased mutations
tasks.description: convex/foodLibrary.ts mergeDuplicates (~L299-854) is a public mutation that collects all rows from 7 tables in one transaction and makes N individual ctx.db.patch() calls. Risks exceeding Convex per-mutation limits for active users. Fix: (1) Convert mergeDuplicates to an action that orchestrates multiple internal mutations. (2) Each phase handles one table: collect rows matching the merge criteria, then patch in batches of ~100. (3) Add row-count caps to each collect. Read convex/_generated/ai/guidelines.md first.
acceptanceCriteria: mergeDuplicates is an action orchestrating phased internal mutations
Each phase handles one table with batched patches
Row-count caps prevent unbounded collects
Typecheck passes
Existing foodLibrary tests pass

tasks.title: Optimize listFoodEmbeddings staleness check — avoid reading full vectors
tasks.description: listFoodEmbeddings in convex/foodParsing.ts (~L453-470) fetches up to 1,000 full foodEmbeddings documents (each ~12KB with 1536 floats) just to check canonicalName and embeddingSourceHash for staleness. Fix: OPTION A (preferred, no new table): Add a .withIndex query that returns only the metadata fields needed. Use a Convex query with field selection if available, or restructure the staleness check to use a lightweight metadata query. OPTION B (needs user approval — new table): Create a foodEmbeddingMeta table with only canonicalName and embeddingSourceHash. NOTE: Per PRD FR-04 (no new tables), prefer Option A. If Option A is infeasible, flag for user decision.
acceptanceCriteria: Staleness check does not read full 1536-float embedding vectors
Read volume reduced from ~12MB to ~50KB for 1000 entries
No new Convex table created unless explicitly approved by user
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Replace per-row setInterval in RelativeTime with global hook
tasks.description: columns.tsx (~L141-153) creates one setInterval per visible table row (25-100 timers). Fix: (1) Create a useCurrentMinute() hook that returns a timestamp that updates every 60 seconds via a single global interval. Use a module-level listener pattern so all instances share one timer. (2) Replace the per-instance setInterval in RelativeTime with useCurrentMinute(). (3) Move formatRelativeTime to src/lib/dateUtils.ts (it doesn't belong in a column-factory file).
acceptanceCriteria: No setInterval inside RelativeTime cell renderer
useCurrentMinute hook creates one global interval shared across instances
formatRelativeTime moved to src/lib/dateUtils.ts

tasks.title: Optimize ProfileContext — remove JSON.stringify comparison
tasks.description: ProfileContext.tsx (~L138-188) calls JSON.stringify(nextProfile) on every render to detect changes. patchProfile manually wraps 10 fields in conditional spread. Fix: (1) Replace JSON.stringify deep comparison with a shallow field-by-field check (compare each top-level field). (2) Replace the 10-field manual spread with Object.fromEntries(Object.entries(updates).filter(([, v]) => v !== undefined)).

tasks.title: Debounce Patterns.tsx localStorage writes and add runtime validation
tasks.description: Patterns.tsx (~L43-52, L76-116, L156-189) has three useEffect hooks that write to localStorage on every state change (filters, sorting, views). localStorage.setItem is synchronous blocking I/O. Also uses `as` type assertions on JSON.parse output from untrusted data. Fix: (1) Debounce all three localStorage write effects (300ms). Use a simple debounce pattern (setTimeout + clearTimeout in cleanup). (2) Replace `as` casts on JSON.parse output with proper runtime validation (check shape before assignment, fall back to defaults on invalid data).
acceptanceCriteria: All three localStorage write effects are debounced (300ms)
No `as` cast on JSON.parse output — runtime validation used instead
Invalid localStorage data falls back to defaults
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Fix Confetti onComplete re-trigger via useRef
tasks.description: Confetti.tsx (~L94) includes onComplete in useEffect deps. If the parent passes an unstable (non-memoized) onComplete, the effect re-fires every render, potentially restarting confetti animation. Fix: Use a useRef to hold the onComplete callback so it is not a dependency: `const onCompleteRef = useRef(onComplete); onCompleteRef.current = onComplete;` then use onCompleteRef.current in the effect and remove onComplete from deps.
acceptanceCriteria: onComplete held in useRef, not in effect deps
Effect does not re-fire when parent re-renders
Typecheck passes

tasks.title: Replace E2E waitForTimeout with condition waits and extract page objects
tasks.description: 60 waitForTimeout calls across 11 E2E spec files use hardcoded ms delays. Under CI load delays are insufficient; locally they waste time. Sleep-tracking spec has a retry loop masking failures. Each spec re-defines navigation/locator helpers. Fix: (1) Replace every page.waitForTimeout(N) with explicit condition waits: `await expect(element).toBeVisible({ timeout: 8000 })` or `await element.waitFor({ state: 'visible', timeout: 5000 })`. (2) Extract shared page objects (navigateAndWait, getNutritionCard, getBowelSection, getQuickCapture) into e2e/fixtures.ts or e2e/pages/. (3) Remove retry loops in sleep-tracking spec — use single attempts with appropriate timeouts. IMPORTANT: Run the full E2E suite after updating each spec file, not as a batch. Files with waitForTimeout (by count): nutrition-water-modal (18), fluid-tracking (10), patterns-food-trials (6), food-pipeline (5), drpoo-cooldown (5), weight-tracking (4), food-tracking (3), destructive-habits (3), bowel-tracking (3), nutrition-full-flow (2), nutrition-calorie-detail (1).
acceptanceCriteria: Zero page.waitForTimeout calls in E2E specs (grep verification)
All waits use explicit conditions (toBeVisible, waitFor, etc.)
Shared page objects extracted to e2e/fixtures.ts or e2e/pages/
No retry loops in sleep-tracking spec
Navigation/locator helpers not duplicated across specs
All E2E tests pass

verification.testPolicy: Tests must be updated to reflect new behavior. Never simplify or remove assertions to make tests pass — update them to match the new reality.

tasks.title: Make getWeekStart deterministic — pure epoch arithmetic in shared/
tasks.description: getWeekStart in convex/computeAggregates.ts (~L426-441) uses Date mutation methods with implicit timezone behavior inside an internalMutation. Client and server may compute different week boundaries. Fix: (1) Rewrite getWeekStart as pure epoch arithmetic (no Date constructor, no setHours/setDate). (2) Move to shared/ so both client and server import the same function. (3) Accept a timezone offset parameter or use UTC consistently. (4) Update both convex/computeAggregates.ts and convex/aggregateQueries.ts to use the shared function.
acceptanceCriteria: getWeekStart is a pure function in shared/ (no Date mutation methods)
Both client and server import the same function
No timezone-dependent behavior (UTC or explicit offset)
Client and server compute identical week boundaries for same input
Typecheck passes
All aggregate tests pass

tasks.title: Fix stale period bounds in weekly summary auto-trigger
tasks.description: getCompletedPeriodBounds() is called inside useMemo(() => ..., []) with empty deps — computed once and never updated. If the app stays open across a half-week boundary, the hook never triggers for the new period. Fix: Track the boundary using a state variable that updates via setTimeout (schedule next boundary) or visibilitychange listener (recalculate when tab becomes visible).

tasks.title: Standardize day-boundary strategy in Bristol/BmFrequency tiles
tasks.description: BmFrequencyTile uses calendar midnight boundaries while BristolTrendTile uses raw millisecond arithmetic. A log at 11:59 PM may be included in one but excluded from the other. Both compute Date.now() outside memos. Fix: (1) Standardize on calendar midnight approach. (2) Extract shared getCutoffTimestamp(daysAgo: number, nowMs: number) utility. (3) Accept a nowMs prop from a parent that controls the clock. (4) Combine chained useMemo calls where deps overlap.
acceptanceCriteria: Both tiles use calendar midnight boundaries
